title: Error relocating ... RELRO protection failed: No error information (SELinux related)
body:
### Issue Description

A simple start of the container fails with
```
Error relocating /lib/ld-musl-x86_64.so.1: RELRO protection failed: No error information
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

There are differences in running different versions of rclone/rclone from docker.io (and ghcr.io)

[docker.io/rclone/rclone:1.72.0](https://hub.docker.com/layers/rclone/rclone/1.72.0/images/sha256-d7f6732d099293ad053af4ae67b5de4a85edc9e03c58ed6cbc0ecb02c1293afa) vs [docker.io/rclone/rclone:v1.72-stable](https://hub.docker.com/layers/rclone/rclone/v1.72-stable/images/sha256-aa945cde89f5d50cb7b9efddd0b9d9f5d2bb75db8d771d54d748564db884c2cd)

rclone/rclone:1.72.0 adds a layer 
`1 ADD alpine-minirootfs-3.22.2-x86_64.tar.gz / # buildkit`
while rclone/rclone:v1.72-stable (1.72.1 probably) adds
 `1 ADD alpine-minirootfs-3.23.0-x86_64.tar.gz / # buildkit`

which runs into unexpected behavior - Steps to reproduce the issue:

```bash
$ podman run --name rclone-sync-i --replace -ti --rm --entrypoint=ash docker.io/rclone/rclone:1.72.0
/data #
^+D
$ podman run --name rclone-sync-i --replace -ti --rm --entrypoint=ash docker.io/rclone/rclone:v1.72-stable
Error relocating /lib/ld-musl-x86_64.so.1: RELRO protection failed: No error information
Error relocating /bin/ash: RELRO protection failed: No error information
```

```bash
$ sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent
----
time->Sun Jan 11 17:13:02 2026
type=AVC msg=audit(1768147982.779:2219): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=851817 comm="ash" path="/lib/ld-musl-x86_64.so.1" dev="sda5" ino=713576 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c613,c956 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c183,c236 tclass=file permissive=0
----
time->Sun Jan 11 17:13:02 2026
type=AVC msg=audit(1768147982.780:2220): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=851817 comm="ash" path="/bin/busybox" dev="sda5" ino=34303686 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c613,c956 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c183,c236 tclass=file permissive=0
```

### Describe the results you received

It is noteworthy that rclone itself has no problems, only when the shell is required in the quadlet, e.g., in
```
Environment=RCLONE_PASSWORD_COMMAND=“cat /run/secrets/password”
```
the job is then terminated. 


### Describe the results you expected

I would not expect any problems between these version jumps...

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

Newer versions of rclone are also affected.

comments.body: Unable to reproduce this on Fedora rawhide with podman and container-selinux from `rhcontainerbot/podman-next` copr. Trying on fedora-43...

@opelx I'm guessing you have podman 5.7.1 from the official fedora 43 rpm? Also, what's `rpm -q container-selinux` say for you?

EDIT: `rpm -q selinux-policy` as well please.
Unable to reproduce this on vanilla Fedora 43 either. @opelx your `/etc/os-release` says CoreOS, so I wonder if something coreos specific. Let's start with collecting rpm info ^.

Thank you for investigating

```bash
$ rpm -q container-selinux
container-selinux-2.244.0-1.fc43.noarch
$ rpm -q selinux-policy
selinux-policy-42.19-1.fc43.noarch
$ rpm -q podman
podman-5.7.1-1.fc43.x86_64
```

All packages are stock from [coreos stable stream](https://fedoraproject.org/coreos/release-notes?arch=x86_64&stream=stable).
@opelx would you be able to `dnf reinstall container-selinux` on that machine and retry please?

Thank you very much. A simple dnf reinstall will probably not work, as CoreOS mounts /usr read-only and uses ostree for the packages (at least I haven't found anything, I would have to brutally uninstall the package, which would probably trigger some dependencies). It's problematic because I might lose the entire installation. Even though it may be designed as a disposable OS, I haven't used it that way (yet) (there are still important things on it, such as secrets, etc. - I just started with CoreOS and Podman).


```
 dnf repoquery -l container-selinux
Updating and loading repositories:
 Fedora 43 - x86_64 - Updates                                                                                                                                                            100% |   4.2 MiB/s |  80.3 MiB |  00m19s
 Fedora 43 - x86_64                                                                                                                                                                      100% |   2.0 MiB/s |  57.8 MiB |  00m29s
 Fedora 43 openh264 (From Cisco) - x86_64                                                                                                                                                100% | 363.0   B/s |  10.1 KiB |  00m28s
 Fedora 43 - x86_64 - Updates Archive                                                                                                                                                    100% |   2.0 MiB/s |  66.1 MiB |  00m34s
Repositories loaded.
/etc/selinux/targeted/contexts/users/container_u
/usr/share/containers/selinux
/usr/share/containers/selinux/contexts
/usr/share/doc/container-selinux
/usr/share/doc/container-selinux/README.md
/usr/share/man/man8/container_selinux.8.gz
/usr/share/selinux/devel

/usr/share/selinux/devel/include/services/container.if
/usr/share/selinux/packages
/usr/share/selinux/packages/container.pp.bz2
/usr/share/udica
/usr/share/udica/templates
/usr/share/udica/templates/base_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/config_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/home_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/log_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/net_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/tmp_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/tty_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/virt_container.cil
/usr/share/udica/templates/x_container.cil
/var/lib/selinux/mls/active/modules/200/container
/var/lib/selinux/targeted/active/modules/200/container
```

However, I created another Playground CoreOS installation, and the problem doesn't occur there! So something must have been unintentionally stored under /var or something that causes this behavior from SELinux and also survives a reboot. What could it be?
does `restorecon -R -v /var/lib/containers` help ? Also, does this occur with any other image (another alpine version / variant) ?

Unfortunately not. On another CoreOS installation that I set up in the meantime, it works as expected. I can only assume that the SELinux policies are taking effect where they shouldn't. Since I was experimenting with restic backup on this machine, this may have caused these very specific problems.

It has also occurred with some other containers—but not all of them. Unfortunately, I could not identify a pattern.

Nevertheless, thank you for investigating. I will close the issue and also reinstall this CoreOS installation after saving the data. Unfortunately, I am left with an uneasy feeling...
